mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the equity risk premium and discount rates applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightness, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and residential investment indicates robust credit availability and household confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand and lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression predicting 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a late-cycle environment signals capacity utilization approaching maximum levels, which compresses profit margins through input cost inflation and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 60% of observations where industrial production rises while the economy is classified in a late-cycle regime.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation while policy rates remain near zero forces an abrupt monetary policy pivot, which shocks equity valuations downward and produces negative forward returns over a quarterly window.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > -1.5%) when both the inflation acceleration and zero-rate floor conditions are simultaneously met.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth confirms underlying economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The sign of the GDP growth coefficient flips to negative in a rolling 3-year window, or the predictive R-squared drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates broad-based labor demand expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline PAYEMS-only signal in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, with a Diebold-Mariano test p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals deteriorating household wealth and credit conditions, which reduces aggregate consumption and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year backtest.
